content:
# Acts Chapter 10 -- Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Chapter:** Acts 10 (Cornelius, Peter's Vision, Gentiles Receive the Spirit)

| Verse | Issue | EMET | LOGOS | PNEUMA | SOPHIA Resolution |
|-------|-------|------|-------|--------|-------------------|
| 1 | Opening syntax | "And a certain man in Caesarea" | "Now there was a certain man in Caesarea" | "In Caesarea there was a man" | "Now there was a certain man in Caesarea" -- follows LOGOS; preserves tis (certain) and smooth narrative transition |
| 2 | "alms" vs. "acts of charity" | "doing many alms to the people" | "who gave many alms to the people" | "gave generously to those in need" | "giving many alms to the people" -- preserves eleemosynas (alms); PNEUMA's paraphrase loses the technical term |
| 3 | Time reference | "the ninth hour of the day" | "the ninth hour of the day" | "around three o'clock" | "the ninth hour of the day" -- 2-to-1; preserves the Jewish time-reckoning of the source text |
| 4 | "ascended" vs. "gone up" | "gone up for a memorial" | "ascended as a memorial" | "risen before God as an offering he remembers" | "ascended as a memorial" -- follows LOGOS; "ascended" carries sacrificial overtones; PNEUMA over-interprets mnemosynon as "offering" |
| 10 | "trance" vs. "ecstasy" | "an ecstasy fell upon him" | "a trance fell upon him" | "he fell into a trance" | "a trance fell upon him" -- 2-to-1; Greek ekstasis means standing-outside-oneself; "trance" is the standard English equivalent |
| 11 | "vessel" + descriptive noun | "a certain vessel descending, as a great linen-sheet" | "a certain vessel descending like a great sheet" | "something like a great sheet being lowered" | "a certain vessel descending like a great sheet" -- follows LOGOS; preserves skeuos as the noun with othone as comparative |
| 12 | "wild beasts" (kai theria) included? | Omitted (three categories) | Omitted (three categories) | Omitted (three categories) | Omitted -- Sinaiticus reads three categories only (four-footed, creeping things, birds); all three translators agree |
| 14 | "common" vs. "impure" (koinon) | "common and unclean" | "common or unclean" | "impure or unclean" | "common or unclean" -- follows LOGOS; koinon (ritually profane) and akatharton (ceremonially impure) are distinct categories; PNEUMA collapses the distinction by rendering both as near-synonyms |
| 15 | "make common" vs. "call common" vs. "call impure" | "you must not make common" | "you must not call common" | "you must not call impure" | "you must not call common" -- follows LOGOS; the declarative force of koinou is captured by "call"; "common" preserves the key term |
| 19 | Number of men (critical variant) | "men" (no number -- notes Sinaiticus lacks numeral) | "three men" | "three men" | "men" (no number) -- follows EMET; Sinaiticus original hand appears to lack the numeral TREIS; the narrative context (v. 7: two servants + one soldier) makes three men clear without requiring the number in the text |
| 21 | "Behold" included | "Behold, I am the one whom you are seeking" | "Behold, I am the one whom you are seeking" | "I am the one you are looking for" | "Behold, I am the one whom you are seeking" -- 2-to-1; preserves idou and formal relative clause |
| 22 | "testified-to" vs. "well spoken of" vs. "respected" | "being testified-to by" | "well spoken of by" | "respected by" | "well spoken of by" -- follows LOGOS; martyroumenon means "attested/testified about" |
| 25 | "worshiped" (prosekynesen) | "worshiped" | "worshiped" | "fell at his feet to worship him" | "worshiped" -- all agree on the verb; Peter's correction in v. 26 confirms full force; SOPHIA preserves the simple aorist |
| 28 | "of another nation" vs. "Gentile" | "one of-another-nation" | "one of another nation" | "a Gentile" | "one of another nation" -- follows LOGOS; allophylos is more precise than ethnos; PNEUMA's "Gentile" substitutes a broader theological category |
| 28 | "common or unclean" applied to persons | "common or unclean" | "common or unclean" | "impure or unclean" | "common or unclean" -- 2-to-1; same koinon/akatharton distinction as v. 14, now applied to persons -- the theological pivot of the chapter |
| 30 | "fasting and praying" (NESTEUON) | Omitted | Included | Omitted | Included -- Sinaiticus appears to include NESTEUON; SOPHIA follows the manuscript reading with LOGOS |
| 30 | "bright clothing" vs. "dazzling clothes" | "bright clothing" | "bright clothing" | "dazzling clothes" | "bright clothing" -- 2-to-1; Greek lampra means shining/bright; "dazzling" over-intensifies |
| 33 | "by the Lord" vs. "by God" (critical variant) | "by the Lord" (hypo tou kyriou) | "by the Lord" | "the Lord has commanded" | "by the Lord" -- Sinaiticus reads KYRIOU, not THEOU; potentially higher Christological weight; all three follow this reading |
| 34 | "respecter-of-persons" vs. "shows partiality" vs. "favoritism" | "God is not a respecter-of-persons" | "God is not one who shows partiality" | "God does not show favoritism" | "God is not one who shows partiality" -- follows LOGOS; preserves the noun prosopolemptes; EMET is archaic, PNEUMA too colloquial |
| 36-37 | Anacoluthon handling | Preserves rough syntax literally with minimal smoothing | Parenthetical with em-dashes; colon transition | Smoothed into natural English; syntactic break eliminated | Follows LOGOS approach with double-dashes for parenthetical; preserves the syntactic break as a feature of Peter's passionate oral delivery rather than smoothing it away |
| 36 | "sons of Israel" vs. "people of Israel" | "sons of Israel" | "sons of Israel" | "people of Israel" | "sons of Israel" -- 2-to-1; huiois Israēl is literally "sons of Israel" |
| 36 | "Lord of all" parenthetical placement | Parenthetical with dashes | Parenthetical with em-dash | Set off with em-dash | Double-dashes: "this one is Lord of all" -- all three agree on parenthetical treatment; SOPHIA uses double-dash convention |
| 39 | "tree" vs. "cross" (xylon) | "tree" | "tree" | "cross" | "tree" -- 2-to-1; xylon means wood/tree and deliberately echoes Deuteronomy 21:22-23 (LXX curse formula); "cross" loses this allusion |
| 40 | "gave him to become manifest" vs. "granted" | "gave him to become manifest" | "granted that he should become manifest" | "made him visible" | "granted that he should become manifest" -- follows LOGOS; EMET is overly literal; PNEUMA loses the divine permission nuance of edōken |
| 41 | "chosen beforehand" construction | "witnesses having been chosen-beforehand by God" | "witnesses who had been chosen beforehand by God" | "witnesses chosen by God beforehand" | "witnesses who had been chosen beforehand by God" -- follows LOGOS; English relative clause preserves procheirotoneō clearly |
| 45 | "believers from among the circumcision" | "faithful ones of the circumcision" | "believers from among the circumcision" | "Jewish believers" | "believers from among the circumcision" -- follows LOGOS; preserves Luke's sociological precision; PNEUMA loses the specific phrase ek peritomēs |
| 45 | "astonished" vs. "stunned" | "were astonished" | "were astonished" | "were stunned" | "were astonished" -- 2-to-1; exestēsan; both valid, but "astonished" matches the formal register |
| 47 | Rhetorical question phrasing | "Surely no one is able to forbid the water" | "Can anyone withhold the water so that these should not be baptized" | "Can anyone withhold the water for baptizing these people?" | "Can anyone withhold the water so that these should not be baptized" -- follows LOGOS; preserves the mē construction and the infinitive of result |
| 48 | "some days" vs. "a few days" | "some days" | "some days" | "a few days" | "some days" -- 2-to-1; hēmeras tinas is indefinite; "a few" implies a specific small number not warranted by the Greek |

-- SOPHIA
